title: Learning properties of ordered and disordered materials from multi-fidelity data
methods: data scaling analysis (varying N_PBE and N_high-fi)
data_sources: Castelli et al. via MPContribs (GLLB-SC, 2290 crystals)
Zhuo et al. experimental band gaps (2981 crystals)
Inorganic Crystal Structure Database (ICSD) for structure cross-reference
QM9 and QM7b molecular benchmark datasets

data_scope.data_accessed: PBE: 2019-06-01; ICSD cross-referenced for experimental structures

core_conclusion: Multi-fidelity graph networks (MEGNet) achieve 22-45% lower mean absolute error on experimental band gap predictions by jointly training on large cheap PBE data and scarce high-fidelity HSE/experimental data. Trainable elemental embeddings allow disordered crystal sites to be represented as occupancy-weighted linear combinations, extending graph network prediction to the majority of known crystals that exhibit site disorder.
why_it_matters: Overcomes the data-scarcity bottleneck for high-accuracy materials property prediction; first ML framework to handle disordered crystals at scale (>120,000 ICSD entries); provides mechanistic insight via latent feature geometry; outperforms transfer learning and model stacking; releases open data and code as community infrastructure.
why_nature: Universal framework applicable beyond band gaps to any property and molecules; addresses a fundamental architectural gap (multi-fidelity + disorder) rather than incrementally improving a single benchmark; rigorous comparison to all competing paradigms; dual open data+code contribution matches Nature Computational Science's reproducibility mandate.
added_value_to_my_research: Continuous fidelity encoder for multi-source GeoAI fusion (GPS, mobile, remote sensing as fidelity tiers)
Active fidelity selection via uncertainty head -> principled field survey vs. remote sensing trade-off for health geography
Elemental embedding interpolation -> mixed land-use zone representation in spatial graph networks for urban analytics
Latent feature geometry as explainability -> grounding physics-informed graph learning interpretability in spatial prediction
Multi-fidelity graph network template directly applicable to satellite + survey + census tiers for accessibility prediction in data-sparse regions

related.title: Data as the next challenge in atomistic machine learning
related.relation: discusses data quality, quantity, and multi-fidelity challenges in atomistic ML — the central problem this paper solves

related.title: Uncertainty-driven dynamics for active learning of interatomic potentials
related.relation: active learning with uncertainty quantification for high-fidelity ML — complements multi-fidelity data acquisition strategy

related.title: A universal graph deep learning interatomic potential for the periodic table
related.relation: universal graph network for all elements — extends the MEGNet elemental embedding paradigm to interatomic potentials

related.title: Molecular contrastive learning of representations via graph neural networks
related.relation: contrastive graph representation learning for molecules — alternative paradigm for learning chemically meaningful latent spaces

related.title: Accelerating the prediction of stable materials with machine learning
related.relation: ML-accelerated materials stability prediction using structural databases — adjacent application of graph ML to crystal property prediction

related.title: Deep-learning density functional theory Hamiltonian for efficient ab initio electronic-structure calculation
related.relation: deep learning to emulate DFT calculations — converging direction with multi-fidelity approach to bridge computational and experimental accuracy

figures.caption: Multi-fidelity materials graph network architecture: atoms as nodes, bonds as edges, fidelity level encoded as a global state embedding; sequential graph convolution layers update atom, bond, and state features
Test MAEs for multi-fidelity models on ordered crystal band gaps across fidelity combinations (1-fi to 5-fi) and as a function of N_PBE data size, showing 22-45% MAE reduction vs. 1-fi baselines
t-SNE projections of latent structural features for 1-fi vs. 2-fi models, and band-gap difference vs. normalized feature distance plots showing near-linear correspondence in the 2-fi model
Disordered multi-fidelity model predictions on AlGaN, ZnCdSe, MgZnO alloy band gaps and LuGaAlO4 garnet Eg shift as a function of composition variable x
